forecaster_note: Small surf but worth a paddle with all-day offshores — crisp and clean. Getting into the waist-to-chest range as the SE swell eases right back; biggest in the morning. Clean and well shaped all day with breezy offshores — pack some extra foam and maybe take a big board out to make the most of it. INCOMING: Weds to the weekend, the next SE–E swell fills in for another round of decent to maybe solid size. — Anthony Spinks (Surfline)

wind.summary: NW 21kph, gusting to 48 — straight offshore at all four spots, all day. Groomed and clean, just punchy gusts on the paddle-out.

shark.summary: Shark observed by SLS drone at Long Reef 10:32am Saturday — water was evacuated. Only Northern Beaches detection in the last 24h. Stay sharp at Long Reef, surf with company.

days.label: Today
days.note: Offshore all day and clean — a small-but-fun Sunday. Biggest in the morning as the SE swell eases; your 10–12 slot rides the drop from the 6:18am high to the 11:50am low. Bring volume. 🦈 Drone spotted a shark at Long Reef yesterday morning (water evacuated) — buddy up if you surf there.

dee-why.window: Morning is the call — surf the drop from the 6:18am high toward the 11:50am low. Mid-morning mid-tide should have the banks working.

tip.label: Point call
tip.text: 92kJ at 65/100 — waist–chest and groomed. ESE 116° is a friendly angle with zero dead-S wrap, but the Point will be soft at this size — frontside rights off the beachie banks are the better bet.

long-reef.window: Biggest and best-rated of the four — the reefs sweep up the leftover ESE groundswell. Morning through midday on the dropping tide.
alerts: 🦈 Shark observed by SLS drone 10:32am Sat — water was evacuated. Surf with company and check with lifeguards

tip.label: Best bet
tip.text: Fair–good, 115kJ, chest–head and dead offshore — the size-holder today. Weigh it against yesterday morning's drone sighting: go with a mate, don't dawn-patrol it solo.

curl-curl.window: Clean peaks on the morning tide drop — holds a touch more push than DY and no shark flags here.

tip.label: Plan B
tip.text: 101kJ at 66/100 — the most consistent beachie option. Pick your bank: backside lefts or frontside rights both on offer, empty early.

manly.window: Smallest of the four — tidy little peelers for a longboard or groveler cruise.

tip.label: Skip
tip.text: Thigh–stomach and clean, but Long Reef, Curly and DY all beat it for push this morning.

days.label: Tomorrow
days.note: Likely small to tiny but clean — traces of swell and breezy offshores. Rest day, or a longboard glide at whichever bank still bends. The next SE–E swell arrives Wednesday.

dee-why.window: Tiny — log it or skip it.

long-reef.window: Only spot holding rideable size — the reefs milk what's left of the ESE energy.

days.label: Weekend
days.note: Next weekend: leftovers of Wednesday's SE–E swell keep 2–3ft in the water with decent shape likely as it slowly eases — Sunday looks a touch bigger than Saturday at the exposed spots. If Wed–Thu delivers (Long Reef charts show 4–5ft), next weekend is worth planning around.

dee-why.window: Easing SE–E swell, winter morning offshores likely — mid-morning window should work both days.

long-reef.window: Best size-holder again — Sunday 19th showing 2–3ft as the mid-week swell eases through.

curl-curl.window: S-swell magnet keeps the peaks rolling — 2–3ft Sunday on the charts.

manly.window: Rideable both days, bigger Sunday — the protected option if the swell overdelivers mid-week.
